LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SHANE BANNING'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER, APPOINTMENT OF DISCOVERY REFEREE AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF DISCOVERY REFEREE IS GRANTED. BOTH PARTIES TO SHARE THE COST OF THE REFEREE. PARTIES SHOULD MEET AND CONFER TO AGREE ON A REFEREE. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A REFEREE WITHIN 15 DAYS, EACH SIDE SHOULD SUBMIT A LIST OF THREE NAMES TO THE COURT FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION AND DESIGNATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER). AS TO THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS, THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Pltf For A Protective Order, Appointment Of Discovery Referee And Requet For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF SHANE BANNING'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER, APPOINTMENT OF DISCOVERY REFEREE AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Rory C. Quintana, Esq. of Kumin Sommers LLP 434-4500 for pltf & moving party; Michael McCloskey, Esq. 572-4257 for deft & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF DISCOVERY REFEREE IS GRANTED. BOTH PARTIES TO SHARE THE COST OF THE REFEREE. PARTIES SHOULD MEET AND CONFER TO AGREE ON A REFEREE. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A REFEREE WITHIN 15 DAYS, EACH SIDE SHOULD SUBMIT A LIST OF THREE NAMES TO THE COURT FOR THE COURT'S CONSIDERATION AND DESIGNATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.)
As to the request for sanctions, the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Request for sanctions denied. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Off Calendar Aug-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT EVERT GROBBELAAR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT, DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND FOR LEAVE TO DEFEND THE ACTION is granted. Within 10 days of notice of entry of this order, defendant to file an answer complying with the terms of the previous demurrer and order: pleading facts to support the alleged defenses. Defendant's former counsel, Brant Girard, to pay reasonable attorney's fees and costs associated with the default, collection efforts and response to this motion in the amount of $13,514.30. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2010 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Stephen Sommers, Esq. and Rory Quintana, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Shane Banning, an individual, who was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Evert Grobbelaar, an individual.
Default was entered agasint defendant on October 29, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Shane Banning, an individual, and against the Defaulted Defendant Evert Grobbelaar, an individual, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $206,000.00.
Punitive Damages - $206,000.00.
Costs - $1,977.03, for a total judgment in the amount of $413,977.03.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
